characters.name: Andrea
characters.description: AndreaPersonal InformationGender:FemaleCharacter description:A wealthy woman who wantsBeckyto have her surrogate daughter.RelationshipsSpouse(s):RichardRoseanneCharacter InformationSeason:10First Episode:"Twenty Years to Life"Last Episode:"Eggs Over, Not Easy"Total Episodes:3The ConnersCharacter InformationSeason:1Episode:"One Flew Over the Conners' Nest"Overal Character InformationTotal Episodes:4Played by:Sarah ChalkeAndrea(portrayed by Sarah Chalke) is a wealthy woman who is willing to payBecky Conner$50,000 to be her surrogate. Becky is chosen as a result of their strong resemblance– Chalke had replacedLecy Goransonas Becky from the6th Seasonto the9th Seasonof the original series. She has a strong dislike of plastic bottles which harm the environment. After Becky gets herself checked out by a Doctor, it is found out that Becky's eggs are too old. This sees very little chances of her being able to have a baby, Andrea thanks her but is left with no choice other than to leave Becky and find someone else to be her surrogate. The next time Andrea is seen, she has had her baby but expresses regret as the baby is too much effort. It is implied she has remained in contact with Becky since the surrogate situation, but for some reason has blocked Jackie on Facebook. Andrea is a young and lively young woman who comes around by herself or bringing her baby to every place she goes. She is very friendly, nice and pleasant. She seems to be happy to pick Becky as her surrogate.

characters.name: Anne-Marie Mitchell
characters.description: Anne-Marie MitchellPersonal InformationGender:FemaleOccupation:Unknown[1]Character description:Highschool friend of Roseanne and Jackie who often gets together with them to gossip about the other neighbors and other accquaintancesRelationshipsSpouse(s):Chuckie MitchellRelatives:1 SonGrandchildrenRoseanneCharacter InformationSeasons:3–5, 8–10First Episode:"Bird is the Word"Last Episode:"Go Cubs"Total Episodes:16Played by:Adilah Barnes Anne-Marie Mitchellis a highschool friend of Roseanne Conner who appears in a recurring basis on Roseanne, appearing in fifteen episodes of the series. The part of Anne-Marie is played by veteran stage/TV/film actressAdilah Barnes. Anne-Marie, who has a young son (that grew up and moved to Omaha), went to high school with Roseanne and Jackie. She reconnects with Roseanne after Becky is in suspended from school for giving the bird in a school photo. Her husbandChuckie(played byJames Pickens, Jr.), is one of Dan Conner's poker playing buddies who also worked with him for a time doing construction as well as helps remodels homes and sells them for a profit. A long time friend of Roseanne and Jackie, oftentimes they and their other girlfriends get together, usually at Roseanne's and "chew the fat" and gossip about people which they all know, and sometimes get into mischief; as in the episode "Pretty in Black" in Season 5, where Anne-Marie, Jackie, Nancy, and Crystal, in a mischievous game of "Truth or Dare: all "dare" Roseanne to take off her bra in public, which she winds up doing on the Conner's back yard, while Dan was in the garage with new next door neighborTy Tilden(Wings Hauser), the father ofMolly Tildenand her sisterCharlotte.

characters.name: Arnie Thomas
characters.description: Arnie ThomasPersonal InformationGender:MaleCharacter description:One of Dan's poker buddies who claimed to be abducted by aliens, leaves wife Nancy Bartlett in Season 3, returns in Season 5 to try to win her back.RelationshipsSpouse(s):Nancy Bartlett(Season 3–4)RoseanneCharacter InformationSeasons:2–5First Episode:"Five of a Kind"Last Episode:"Stand on Your Man"Total Episodes:19Played by:Tom ArnoldArnold Shemp "Arnie" Thomaswas a poker-playing buddy ofDan Connerand the ex-boyfriend ofNancy Bartlett(played bySandra Bernhard) onRoseanne. Although they later married, they later divorced under less than amicable conditions. He later returns to ask her to remarry her in Season 5 after leaving her in Season 3. He was played by comedianTom Arnold,Roseanne Barr's real-life husband at the time. He appears in 22 episodes between Seasons 2 and Season 5. Arnie is the overweight, sometimes hot-headed, jovial friend of Dan Conner. Originally, he was written as a relative stranger to both Roseanne and Jackie, but this was later retconned and it is revealed he went to high school at the same time with them so perhaps they initially just didn't remember him. He frequently cheats on the women with whom he dates, and personality-wise, he is generally very ill-mannered and uncouth in how he responds to others. However, Arnie always tries to be a good friend to Dan. He eventually borrows money from Dan to get Nancy breast implants. A revelation that frustrates Roseanne because she and Dan are often struggling. Although Arnie eventually marries Nancy and takes her with Roseanne and Dan on a trip to Las Vegas, he later leaves her, claiming to have been abducted by aliens. (This preposterous claim is later dramatized in the fourth-season finale end-credit tag, where he is seen conversing with aliens on a spaceship, revealing it possibly actually happened.) Before Arnie and Nancy were engaged, he had a one-night stand with Jackie while she was intoxicated. He is often seen wearing a yellow University of Iowa sweatshirt. Which is possibly second hand as he has never mentioned having attended the university. (In reality, Tom Arnold had attended the University of Iowa in real life) He is last seen in Season 5, where he unsuccessfully tries to win Nancy back. Even after finding out that she is a lesbian.

Please!Last Episode:SecretsTotal Episodes:3Played by:Dan ButlerArthur "Art"is a resident of Lanford and an acquaintance ofDanandRoseannewho made appearances as a minor character in 3 episodes of the 4th season. He was portrayed by actorDan Butler. Art is first introduced when he stops by Lanford Custom Cycles and is coerced into buying a motorcycle by Roseanne andJackie. He later helps the Conner's with their tax returns and gives Dan advice on how to make the cycle shop more profitable. Roseanne attempts to set Jackie up with Art, but Jackie is not interested as she says he is not her type. Despite this, Art does attempt to ask her to dinner although she is not receptive even though she thought he was a nice guy.

characters.name: Barbara Healy
characters.description: Barbara HealyPersonal InformationGender:FemaleNationalityAmericanEthnicityCaucasianCharacter description:A drunk, careless, rough, abusive mother of David and Mark.RelationshipsSpouse(s):Mr. HealyRelatives:Mr. Healy(Ex-Husband)David Healy(Son)Mark Healy(Son)Lisa Healy(Daughter)Nicki Healy(Daughter)Darlene Conner(Daughter-In-Law)Becky Conner(Daughter-In-Law)Hometown:Lanford, Illinois(Formerly)MinnesotaRoseanneCharacter InformationSeason:5First Episode:"It's No Place Like Home for The Holidays"Last Episode:"It's a Boy!"Total Episodes:2Played by:Sally KirklandBarbara Healyis the unstable, alcoholic, and abusive mother ofMark,David, Lisa, and Nicki Healy. She was portrayed by actressSally Kirkland. Barbara is the mother of 4 children;Mark,David, Lisa and Nicki Healy. She is an unstable, alcoholic and abusive mother, especially towards David. When she first appeared, it is heavily implied Barbara is hiding something, possibly an extramarital affair, from her family and husband when she first appears. She claimed to be at her sister Maggie's house even though David proves this to be untrue when David tells Barbara that Maggie had been looking for her. Despite her actions, she refers to Darlene as "Conner trash" and "another slut like her sister before slapping David for questioning her whereabouts and storming off. Later, after separating from her husband, she makes preparations to move to Michigan while he moves to Minnesota with Nicki and Lisa. David doesn't want to move with her because of her abusive ways and him and Darlene attempt to convince her parents to let him move in with them. At first, Roseanne and Dan initially tell Darlene and David they would not be able to take David in and he will have to move with his mother.

characters.name: Brian Cayman
characters.description: Brian was a student in high school and one of Roseanne's employers. A young caucasian male that tries to act much older than he is, and believes he can solve any problem. Brian is career oriented, loves fancy cars, yet does not do that good in school. He is very unpopular, and was called "doofy" byBeckyand a "maggot" by Roseanne. He hardly has any empathy, is an unethical supervisor, and does not hesitate to try stabbing people in the back. It is possible he has lackluster family life, because Roseanne was advised to treat him as if he was her son. Roseanne hoped reaching out to him might overhaul the workplace environment. characters.name: Leon Carp
characters.description: Leon CarpPersonal InformationGender:MaleOccupation:Roseanne's boss at Rodbell's Luncheonette who later works with Roseanne at her and Jackie's restuarant, The Lanford LunchboxCharacter description:A conscientious gay who later marries Scott. A good friend and former boss of Roseanne.RelationshipsSpouse(s):ScottDomestic partner(s):Jerry Gimble(ex-boyfriend)RoseanneCharacter InformationSeasons:3–9First Episode:"Valentine's Day"Last Episode:"Into That Good Night, Part 2"Total Episodes:46Played by:Martin Mull Leon Carp, Roseanne's boss and male best friend onRoseannefrom Seasons 3–9. The part of Leon was played by the late veteran actor/comedianMartin Mull. AsRoseanne'sboss and later co-worker; he starts out as her manager at the Rodbell's Luncheonette, where his obsession with pleasing the customers and by-the-book approach to all rules and schedules quickly gets on her nerves. Leon, correspondingly, soon becomes irate over her sloppy nature and deliberate ignorance of almost everything he tells her to do.  However, they quickly develop a gradual respect and friendship for one another. While he is conscientious, good-hearted, and well-intentioned, Roseanne and Leon have a habit of jokingly insulting each other and talk about their faults, such as when Leon stole Roseanne's tips when she was a waitress. Leon also later becomes tentative friends with Dan; the two seldom interact outside Dan's poker games, but there is nothing to make them hostile, as both of them are intimidated by Roseanne. When the restaurant is closed following Leon's promotion, he, Roseanne and Bonnie bid one another a (grudgingly) fond farewell.

characters.name: Charlotte Tilden
characters.description: Charlotte TildenPersonal InformationGender:FemaleCharacter description:Sweet innocent girl, unlike her sister,Molly, whose moves next door.RelationshipsRelatives:Ty Tilden(Father)Molly Tilden(Sister; deceased)RoseanneCharacter InformationSeason:5First Episode:"Pretty in Black"Last Episode:"Promises, Promises"Total Episodes:5Played by:Mara HobelCharlotte Tildenis the next door neighbor to the Conner Family inSeason 5, appearing in five episodes in total. She was portrayed by actressMara Hobel. Charlotte is the pleasant and more reserved daughter ofTy Tilden. Unlike her younger sister,Molly, Charlotte is a by-the-book rule follower who is easily walked all over. A viewer could make some parallels in the Charlotte-Molly dynamic to  Roseanne-Jackie's relationship. One dominate personality who bossed around and gave little thought to the more reserved personality. Charlotte is somewhat socially awkward and typically does not say much about herself. She loves to cook and is seen more than once coming over to visit withRoseanneand the family while offering cookies or rice crispy treats as an excuse to have some company.

characters.description: Chuckie MitchellPersonal InformationGender:MaleOccupation:Mechanic/Factory workerCharacter description:Poker playing buddy of Dan and husband of Anne-Marie, an old childhood friend of Roseanne and JackieRelationshipsSpouse(s):Anne-Marie MitchellRelatives:1 SonGrandchildrenRoseanneCharacter InformationSeasons:3–10First Episode:"Trick or Treat"Last Episode:"Knee Deep"Total Episodes:21The ConnersCharacter InformationSeasons:1–4First Episode:"Keep on Truckin'"Last Episode:"Messy Situation, Miscommunication and Academic Probation"Total Episodes:9Overal Character InformationTotal Episodes:30Played by:James Pickens, Jr. Chuck "Chuckie" Mitchellis the best friend of Dan Conner and husband ofAnne-Marie Mitchella childhood friend of Roseanne Conner, Chuckie appears in a recurring basis on Roseanne, appearing in fifteen episodes of the series. The part of Chuckie is played by veteran stage/TV/film actorJames Pickens, Jr.. Chuck or "Chuckie", is Dan Conner's closest friend, business partner, and poker buddy. He is married toAnne-Marie, a long time friend of Roseanne and Jackie. Usually, when Chuck, Dan and the guys get together, the conversation is usually very light, talking about beer, babes, and at time complain about the wives when they play poker together. But, the tone turns to the issue of race, when D.J. refuses to kiss a black classmate for a play at his school in the episode "White Men Can't Kiss" in Season 7.

Conner(Step Grandson)Jerry Conner(Step Grandson)RoseanneCharacter InformationSeasons:1–6, 8, 10First Episode:"Life and Stuff"Last Episode:"Netflix & Pill"Total Episodes:59The ConnersCharacter InformationSeasons:1, 6First Episode:"Keep on Truckin'"Last Episode:"When Sisters Collide and The Return of the Grifters"Total Episodes:3Overal Character InformationTotal Episodes:62Played by:Natalie WestCrystal Conner (née Anderson)was one of Roseanne and Jackie's best friends who began dating, then married Dan's father, Ed. The part of Crystal is played byNatalie West. Crystal Conner isRoseanne Conner's andJackie Harris' good-hearted childhood friend, a self-described "doormat" when it comes to men. She speaks with a southern accent despite having grown up in Lanford. She always said she got the accent from her father, who was from Arkansas. When she was 16, her mother kicked her out of the house and she moved in with her Aunt Ida. She met her first husband Rusty when she was 17 and divorced at 18. Rusty ended up cutting their trailer in half. She then married a man named Travis and divorced him shortly after. She thought she met the man of her dreams when she met Sonny Anderson, a construction worker. They fell in love and married. While Crystal was pregnant with their son Lonnie, Sonny was out working on the Monument Street Bridge when he slipped off the bridge and fell into one of the pillars as the concrete was being poured. For the longest time, Crystal grieved Sonny's death for several years and couldn't look past it as she raised Lonnie on her own.

Conneris the rambunctious first son of the Conner household on the TV seriesRoseanne. The part of D.J. in the original series was played by then-child actorMichael Fishmanand reprised by Fishman in the revival. The third child of the Conner family and was the youngest before his younger brother, Jerry Garcia, was born. D.J. is the most gullible, naive, and foolish of all the Conner kids. Many jokes are made towards D.J.. The family teases him a lot in the episode, "Homeward Bound" because they discover D.J. has started masturbating. He loves to tease and bother his sisters Becky and Darlene, though deep down, it's obvious the love is there.

characters.name: Dan Conner
characters.description: Dan ConnerPersonal InformationGender:MaleHair Color:Dark blondBorn:(1951-11-10)November 10, 1951(age 74)Occupation:Manager of Lanford City GarageFormer Co-Owner of Lanford Custom CyclesFreelance ContractorO'Linsky'sCharacter description:A funny, caring, sweet, loving, and easygoing family man who works as a drywall contractor.RelationshipsSpouse(s):Roseanne Conner(wife; deceased)Louise Goldufski(wife)Relatives:Ed Conner(Father)Audrey Conner(Mother)Becky Conner(Daughter)Darlene Conner(Daughter)D.J. Conner(Son)Jerry Conner(Son)Harris Healy(Granddaughter)Mark Healy II(Grandson)Mary Conner(Granddaughter)Beverly Rose Conner(Granddaughter)Jackie Harris(Sister-In-Law)Beverly Harris(Mother-In-Law)Nana Mary(Grandmother-In-Law)Andy Harris(Nephew)Crystal Anderson(Step-Mother)Lonnie Anderson(Step-Brother)Ed Conner Jr.(Half-Brother)Angela Conner(Half-Sister)Hometown:Lanford, IllinoisRoseanneCharacter InformationSeasons:1–10First Episode:"Life and Stuff"Last Episode:"Knee Deep"Total Episodes:219The ConnersCharacter InformationSeasons:1–First Episode:"Keep on Truckin'"Last Episode:"The Truck Stops Here"Total Episodes:112Overal Character InformationTotal Episodes:325Played by:John GoodmanAbraham Benrubi(Teenage)Daniel "Dan" Conneris the husband of the lateRoseanne Connerand the father ofBecky,Darlene,D.J., andJerry Conner. He is the deuteragonist of the original series, and the main protagonist of the spin-off. He andLouise Goldufskiget married in season 4 (The Wedding of Dan and Louise) of the spin-off. The part of Dan is played by actorJohn Goodman. Dan was born on November 10, 1951 in Lanford, IL toEd ConnerandAudrey Conner. While Dan was growing up, his father Ed was always on the road as a salesman which put a strain on his and Audrey's marriage. He is a moderately successful drywall worker and a freelance contractor. Occasionally, running into tough times when the construction work slows down, most likely due to the fact that he found most of his job through Dwight's Hardware Store or his lumber yard connections. He met and married his first wifeRoseanne Conner(née Harris) when they were in high school. She was 16 and Dan was 17. Throughout high school, he was a major football star, but unfortunately blew out his knee during a game, so he was not able to get a college football scholarship. During the week him and Roseanne broke up at a dance, he saw Phyllis Zimmer and had a one-night stand with her at the popular make-out spot called Make Out Hill. He never told Roseanne, but she found out from Jackie when they were looking through the yearbook together.

Conner(Son)Jerry Conner(Son)Harris Healy(Granddaughter)Mark Healy II(Grandson)Mary Conner(Granddaughter)Beverly Rose Conner(Granddaughter)Jackie Harris(Sister-In-Law)Beverly Harris(Mother-In-Law)Nana Mary(Grandmother-In-Law)Andy Harris(Nephew)Crystal Anderson(Step-Mother)Lonnie Anderson(Step-Brother)Ed Conner Jr.(Half-Brother)Angela Conner(Half-Sister)Hometown:Lanford, IllinoisRoseanneCharacter InformationSeasons:1–10First Episode:"Life and Stuff"Last Episode:"Knee Deep"Total Episodes:219The ConnersCharacter InformationSeasons:1–First Episode:"Keep on Truckin'"Last Episode:"The Truck Stops Here"Total Episodes:112Overal Character InformationTotal Episodes:325Played by:John GoodmanAbraham Benrubi(Teenage)Daniel "Dan" Conneris the husband of the lateRoseanne Connerand the father ofBecky,Darlene,D.J., andJerry Conner. He is the deuteragonist of the original series, and the main protagonist of the spin-off. He andLouise Goldufskiget married in season 4 (The Wedding of Dan and Louise) of the spin-off. The part of Dan is played by actorJohn Goodman. Dan was born on November 10, 1951 in Lanford, IL toEd ConnerandAudrey Conner. While Dan was growing up, his father Ed was always on the road as a salesman which put a strain on his and Audrey's marriage. He is a moderately successful drywall worker and a freelance contractor. Occasionally, running into tough times when the construction work slows down, most likely due to the fact that he found most of his job through Dwight's Hardware Store or his lumber yard connections. He met and married his first wifeRoseanne Conner(née Harris) when they were in high school. She was 16 and Dan was 17. Throughout high school, he was a major football star, but unfortunately blew out his knee during a game, so he was not able to get a college football scholarship. During the week him and Roseanne broke up at a dance, he saw Phyllis Zimmer and had a one-night stand with her at the popular make-out spot called Make Out Hill. He never told Roseanne, but she found out from Jackie when they were looking through the yearbook together.

She initially took after her father, enjoying sports and manual labor, and was remarked to be Dan Conner's son despite sharing her mother's quick wit and smart mouth; also, like her father, she was a bit lazy and looked for opportunities to dodge major efforts. In the earlier seasons, Darlene was an underachiever at school, often letting her participation in sporting activities take priority over her homework and upsetting teachers by barking in class. Her performance in both basketball and baseball (pitching) was considerable (at least for the first three seasons) but she had to back out of one game mid-round in Season 1 to have her ruptured appendix removed; however, thanks to her contributions her team still won in a landslide.

characters.name: Dean Bates
characters.description: Dean BatesPersonal InformationGender:MaleEthnicityCaucasianHair Color:BrownRoseanneCharacter InformationSeasons:4, 6First Episode:Breaking Up is Hard to DoLast Episode:Don't Ask, Don't TellTotal Episodes:2Played by:David Allan DonahDean Bates, is a student atLanford High Schoolwho appears in two episodes in season 4 and 6. He briefly datedBeckyafter she andMarkhad a falling out. He is portrayed by actor and musicianDavid Allan Donah. Dean is introduced as Becky's rebound date after she and Mark briefly split up when Becky feels like she is not being appreciated. Dan immediately expresses his joy after getting to know Dean and learning how much they have in common. However,RoseanneandJackieimmediately recognize that Dean has no shot at being a long term boyfriend, to Dan's dismay. We see Dean again, years later, when he stops by the Conner's house to pickupDarleneto give her a lift back to college in Chicago, much toDavid's chagrin. They had reconnected after meeting at a frat party. Becky and Mark get into and argument when Becky expresses frustration at Darlene for using her ex-boyfriend.

characters.name: Ed Conner
characters.description: Ed ConnerPersonal InformationGender:MaleDied2020Occupation:Traveling SalesmanCharacter description:A charming, enthusiastic, ambitious, and for the most part likable person and father ofDanRelationshipsSpouse(s):Audrey Conner(Divorced)Crystal AndersonRelatives:Dan Conner(Son)Ed Conner Jr.(Son)Angela Conner(Daughter)Roseanne Conner(Daughter In-Law)Becky Conner(Granddaughter)Darlene Conner(Granddaughter)D.J. Conner(Grandson)Jerry Conner(Grandson)Harris Healy(Great Granddaughter)Mark Healy II(Great Grandson)Mary Conner(Great Granddaughter)Beverly Conner(Great Granddaughter)RoseanneCharacter InformationSeasons:1–3, 6First Episode:"Father's Day"Last Episode:"Lies My Father Told Me"Total Episodes:6Played by:Ned BeattyEdward "Ed" Conner Sr.isDan Conner's father; he appears from seasons 1–3 and 6. The recurring part of Ed Conner is played on the series by the late veteran actorNed Beatty. Ed is a charming and likable traveling salesman who always brings presents for his grandchildren. Dan has a troubled past with his father, but he is well-liked by everyone else. Ed never makes any blatant attempt on the show to anger Dan on purpose, but he often baits his son covertly (e.g., when Ed begins to date Crystal and when Dan questions him on it, Ed makes a rude crack about Dan being "interested" in Crystal, or conning Dan to repair his toaster). It usually doesn't take long for Dan to become annoyed at his father's presence. He is portrayed as somewhat irresponsible and neglectful of his first family, though it is later revealed he justified his actions in reference to raising Dan by reconciling his frequent commitment of Dan's mother to a mental institution with his desire to provide Dan with at least one stable parent as he would often be gone on sales trips during Dan's adolescence. He marries and has two children with Crystal, who is over 20 years his junior.
